title: Post Disaster Housing Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF GLIDE REVITALIZATION MARCH 2025 FLOOD RECOVERY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides long-term recovery assistance for Douglas County residents impacted by the March 2025 flooding. Assistance may include support with housing, home rehabilitation, debris clean up, emergency food, clothing and home furnishings, and mental health services. May be able to refer to home safety inspectors and well water testing assistance. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Residents impacted by the March 2025 flooding Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS CENTER FOR COMMUNITY RESILIENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports those affected by wildfire, including transitioning fire survivors into affordable and permanent housing. Housing case management services look different for each household, but may include: -Advocating for fire-impacted households to help find stable, safe, and affordable housing -Identifying short- and long-term housing needs -Developing individual housing stability plans -Helping program participants conduct housing searches -Providing participants with rent payment and utility bill assistance -Homebuyer counseling services -Homeowner and tenant education opportunities -Collaboration with local partner agencies to provide comprehensive support including disaster relief, mental health, and substance abuse case management 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals and families impacted by 2020 Jackson County wildfires Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 414-0319 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This program is designed to help survivors of specific natural disaster events connect to ODHS Disaster Case Managers. Intake does not guarantee assistance. Disaster case workers may be able to assist individuals in identifying and applying for disaster recovery support or addressing urgent needs. Services will vary based on eligibility and need. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Residents of Oregon who have been affected by natural disasters Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 669-0554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLIDE REVITALIZATION MARCH 2025 FLOOD RECOVERY SUPPORT ACCESS CENTER FOR COMMUNITY RESILIENCE OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE
